fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Alaska Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Alaska - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity alaska, how to transfer pharmacist license to alaska, pharmacist compact alaska, alaska pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: Alaska Board of Pharmacy
board.parent_dept: Division of Corporations, Business and Professional Licensing
board.mailing_address:
State of Alaska
Board of Pharmacy
P.O. Box 110806
Juneau, AK 99811-0806

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Alaska.

steps.title: Create a MyLicense Account and Submit Application
steps.description: Register for an account on MyLicense.Alaska.Gov and complete the online Pharmacist by Endorsement application.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the required non-refundable application fee of $300 through the MyLicense portal.

steps.title: Request Official NABP Transcript
steps.description: Arrange for an official NABP (National Association of Boards of Pharmacy) e-transcript to be sent directly to the Alaska Board of Pharmacy, verifying all previous pharmacist licenses.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow instructions provided after application submission to complete a criminal background check by submitting fingerprints via IdentoGO, using the specific service code for the Division of Corporations, Business and Professional Licensing.

steps.title: Pass Alaska MPJE
steps.description: Schedule and successfully complete the Alaska Multistate Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE). Information on how to schedule will be provided upon receipt of your application.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a Alaska pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does Alaska have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Alaska. To work in Alaska, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Alaska?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Alaska while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in Alaska
hero.dek: To apply for a pharmacist license in Alaska, you must complete the online application through the MyLicense portal, managed by the Division of Corporations, Business and Professional Licensing.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to MyLicense.Alaska.Gov, select 'New User' to create an account, and follow the prompts to register by providing an email and creating a password.
